data.title: TeachTown’s enCORE wins in Primary and Secondary Education at Tech & Learning Awards of Excellence 2025.
data.description: In WOBURN, Massachusetts, as of January 28, 2026, TeachTown, a prominent company offering specialized education curriculum for students with moderate to severe disabilities, is excited to share that their enCORE program, a K-12 standards-based adapted core curriculum, has received recognition as a victor in the Primary category. This program is designed to cater to the unique educational needs of students facing challenges, bringing innovation and success to the forefront.

data.title: NewtekOne to report EPS of $0.66 on Thursday; previous earnings beat led to 3.22% share price drop. Shares at $13.67. Bullish sentiment among long-term shareholders ahead of announcement.
data.description: NewtekOne (NASDAQ: NEWT) will be releasing its quarterly earnings on January 29, 2026. Analysts are expecting an EPS of $0.66. In the previous quarter, the company beat EPS by $0.03, resulting in a 3.22% drop in share price. The stock was trading at $13.67 on January 27, with a 52-week increase of 1.53%. Analysts will be closely watching for positive guidance or forecasted growth for the next quarter. It is important to note that stock price movement can be heavily influenced by guidance rather than earnings beat or miss. Market sentiments and expectations will play a significant role in how investors react to the upcoming earnings release.

data.title: SkyWest (SKYW) to announce Q4 earnings with expected EPS of $2.24. Stock price at $98.2, down 16.14% in 52 weeks. Analysts neutral, $112.0 target.
data.description: SkyWest (NASDAQ: SKYW) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on January 29, 2026. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $2.24. In the last earnings report, the company surpassed EPS expectations by $0.25, resulting in a 5.63% stock price increase. Current stock price is $98.2, down 16.14% over the past year. Market sentiments rate SkyWest as Neutral, with a $112.0 target price, indicating a potential 14.05% increase. Investors are hopeful of positive guidance for the next quarter's performance, emphasizing the impact of future projections on stock prices. Shareholders are cautious given the recent negative returns.

data.title: Lazard (NYSE: LAZ) to report EPS of $0.67 on Jan 29. Past performance shows promising EPS beats. Shares at $52.77, down 5.31% in 52 weeks. Analysts foresee 11.05% upside potential.
data.description: Lazard (LAZ) is set to release its quarterly earnings on 2026-01-29, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $0.67. The market eagerly awaits news of surpassing estimates and positive guidance. In the last quarter, Lazard exceeded EPS estimates by $0.13, resulting in a 0.18% share price increase. In Q3 2025, the EPS was $0.56, compared to an estimated $0.43, causing a price change of 0.00%. As of January 27, Lazard shares were trading at $52.77, down 5.31% over 52 weeks. Analysts rate Lazard as Outperform, with an average one-year price target of $58.6, implying an 11.05% upside potential. Staying abreast of market sentiments and industry expectations is crucial for investors.

data.title: CP Group partners with DRA Advisors to secure relocation of D-Wave to Boca Raton Innovation Campus. Global HQ and R&D facility planned.
data.description: CP Group and DRA Advisors have partnered to secure a significant lease deal with D-Wave Quantum Inc. (NYSE: QBTS), a leading quantum computing firm. D-Wave, known for its dual-platform quantum computing solutions, including annealing and gate-model systems, software, and services, will be relocating its global headquarters to the Boca Raton Innovation Campus. This move will also see the establishment of a prominent U.S. Research and Development (R&D) facility at the campus, marking a key development for the company.

data.title: Eagle Materials (EXP) earnings preview for Jan 29 with analysts predicting EPS of $3.39. Company saw 1.08% stock drop last quarter. Trading at $219.51 with 52-week share decrease of 15.79%. Neutral rating from 7 analysts.

data.title: Mike Dunleavy appointed as CEO of Van Metre Companies to lead long-term growth. Company with 70-year history of success.
data.description: Van Metre Companies, a privately held real estate development, construction, and investment firm, based in Fairfax, Virginia, has recently announced a leadership transition on January 28, 2026. With a history spanning over 70 years, the company has a strong foundation of success. The transition aims to solidify the company's position for long-term growth. This change comes as part of strategic planning to continue the legacy of excellence.

data.title: Tech Alpharetta rebrands to "Tech North Atlanta," expanding footprint to serve the tech community. (Characters: 92)
data.description: Alpharetta, GA - Tech Alpharetta, a nonprofit organization supporting innovation in Alpharetta since 2012, revealed a new name and logo on January 28, 2026. The rebranding signifies an expanded reach to cater to the North Atlanta tech community. This development marks a significant milestone in the organization's evolution, as it continues its mission to foster tech growth. Tech Alpharetta has been a pivotal player in propelling the city to the forefront of innovation, and the new branding is a testament to its commitment to furthering this cause.
